div: Overview Please see use instructions and information at https://sandialabs.github.io/InterSpec/tutorials/#enrichment-and-isotopics-by-nuclides-tool-sep-2025

div.p: The HTML, plain-text, CSV, etc. reports this tool produces — both in the GUI's "Export" menu and from the batch CLI (--file-report-template / --summary-report-template) — are rendered from Inja templates that consume a JSON data model built from the fit solution. You can add your own templates by creating a sub-directory named IsotopicsByNuclidesReportTmplts inside the app's user data directory (see Help→About InterSpec...→Data for this location) and dropping .tmplt.* files into it. Templates placed there become available to the batch CLI by filename.

A complete field reference and authoring guide is shipped at InterSpec_resources/static_text/IsotopicsByNuclidesReportTmplts/README.md. It is written specifically to be pasted into a web-based AI chatbot (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.) along with a plain-English description of the report you want; the chatbot will produce a complete Inja template that you can save into the IsotopicsByNuclidesReportTmplts/ directory above. You can also start from one of the bundled templates in the same directory (std_rel_eff_summary.tmplt.html, std_rel_eff_summary.tmplt.txt, or std_multi_file_summary.tmplt.html) and modify it.
